description: A gathering can disagree and still be 'phaelu' when peace is its sustained disposition rather than a brief lack of agitation. The noun 'shea' is peace itself; 'shena' (calm) stays with the present moment, while 'noalu' (tranquil) describes quiet that has endured.
articulatory_notes: Bring the lips close without sealing them and pass unvoiced air through the gap for 'ph', then open into central 'a'. Move the tongue forward for stressed true-mid unrounded 'e' in its own syllable across the 'a.e' hiatus. Touch the tongue tip to the alveolar ridge for 'l', let the voice pass along its sides, and finish with rounded high-back 'u'.
sound_symbolism: The word begins on breath, opens across the vowel hiatus, and leaves its final airflow unblocked along the sides of the tongue. Its even course offers a felt contour with room for more than one voice.

pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: A peaceful exchange can contain disagreement while keeping coercion and humiliation out of its method. 'phaelu' describes that sustained orientation, not a promise that tension will never arise.

semantic_domains.community: a nonviolent and workable condition among people
semantic_domains.emotion: peacefulness held as a settled disposition
